summary.intent: Fix 3 failing OAuth E2E tests caused by a Playwright strict mode violation, then commit and push the fix.
summary.outcome: Updated the overly broad regex selector in oauth.spec.js to target the modal title specifically using its ID; all 8 OAuth tests now pass and the fix was committed.

repo: msteams is in the default `offered` channels list (schema.rs:1429) alongside telegram, discord, slack, and matrix — available to all fresh installs without config changes.

The OAuth provider modal uses id='providerModalTitle' for its title element, and the model picker UI renders both a modal title ('Select Models') and a separate label ('Select models to add'), making case-insensitive broad regex selectors ambiguous.
PR #529 had two P1 issues fixed: StreamSession retry storm (initial_post_failed flag) and URL injection in search (switched to reqwest .query() builder).

code.finding: getByText(/connected successfully|Select Model/i) matched 2 elements after model picker UI added a 'Select models to add' label alongside the 'Select Models' modal title. Fix: use #providerModalTitle selector or a more specific locator.
Same strict mode violation as line 132 — same fix applied.
Same strict mode violation as lines 132 and 185 — same fix applied.

workflow: Greptile code review was integrated into the PR workflow via a 'greploop' pattern: push → resolve thread → trigger review → poll for completion → verify confidence score before merging.
Playwright strict mode violations (locator resolves to multiple elements) surface as toBeVisible failures and require narrowing selectors to a unique element, often via ID or role combination.

friction: Greptile review required manual polling and multiple tool calls to detect completion; no native wait/webhook mechanism was used.
The OAuth test selector broke silently when new UI elements were added to the model picker — no guard against future ambiguous matches.
open_items: The OAuth test fix uses a narrower selector but the exact replacement isn't shown in the transcript — worth verifying the chosen selector (likely #providerModalTitle) remains stable if the modal is refactored.
Greptile polling loop is manual; consider automating the wait-for-check pattern as a reusable script.
